plan.original_query: What controls apply to API security?

steps.search_query: API security, protection of web APIs and application programming interfaces
steps.purpose: Find controls that require securing, authenticating, authorizing, monitoring, and protecting APIs and web services (e.g., access control, authentication, input validation, logging, encryption, rate limiting).

step.search_query: API security, protection of web APIs and application programming interfaces
step.purpose: Find controls that require securing, authenticating, authorizing, monitoring, and protecting APIs and web services (e.g., access control, authentication, input validation, logging, encryption, rate limiting).

documents.title: Access Only
documents.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

documents.title: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
documents.description:
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to produce a design specification and security and privacy architecture that:
  a. Is consistent with the organization’s security and privacy architecture that is an integral part the organization’s enterprise architecture;
  b. Accurately and completely describes the required security and privacy functionality, and the allocation of controls among physical and logical components; and
  c. Expresses how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together to provide required security and privacy capabilities and a unified approach to protection.

documents.title: Interactive Application Security Testing
documents.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ interactive application security testing tools to identify flaws and document the results.

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Assert and Enforce Application Access
Access Only
documents.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

documents.title: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
documents.description:
Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to produce a design specification and security and privacy architecture that:
  a. Is consistent with the organization’s security and privacy architecture that is an integral part the organization’s enterprise architecture;
  b. Accurately and completely describes the required security and privacy functionality, and the allocation of controls among physical and logical components; and
  c. Expresses how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together to provide required security and privacy capabilities and a unified approach to protection.

documents.title: Interactive Application Security Testing
documents.description: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to employ interactive application security testing tools to identify flaws and document the results.

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Assert and Enforce Application Access

response.answer:
Applicable controls from the supplied set for API security are:

- Require developer-produced security and privacy architecture and design that specifies required security functionality and how controls are allocated and integrated across components, so APIs are designed with explicit security behavior and unified protections [sa-17].

- Require interactive application security testing by the developer to identify and document application-level flaws, which applies to API implementations and helps find API-specific vulnerabilities (eg. injection, auth flaws) during development [sa-11.9].

- Uniquely identify and authenticate system services and applications before establishing communications, which applies directly to APIs and requires that services and applications calling or serving APIs be identified and authenticated prior to interacting [ia-9].

- Require applications to assert the access they need at installation, provide enforcement mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access, and approve access changes after installation. For APIs this means enforcing declared access rights and preventing unauthorized API access or privilege escalation [ac-3.12].

- Use single-device access to allow connections from one device to platforms/applications/data in different security domains while preventing information flow between domains. This can be applied to API gateway or proxy configurations that mediate cross-domain API access and enforce domain separation [ac-4.22].

If you need mapping of specific API controls (e.g., rate limiting, input validation, OAuth scopes) to these items, the supplied controls do not explicitly cover those specifics; they cover design/testing, service authentication, application access assertion/enforcement, and single-device cross-domain access [sa-17][sa-11.9][ia-9][ac-3.12][ac-4.22].

plan.original_query: What policies relate to logging and monitoring?

steps.search_query: Audit logging, event logging, and audit record generation and retention
steps.purpose: Find controls that require generating, protecting, reviewing, and retaining audit logs and events.

steps.search_query: Continuous monitoring, security monitoring, and detection of unauthorized activity
steps.purpose: Identify controls that require ongoing system and security monitoring, intrusion detection, and alerting.

steps.search_query: Log analysis, log management, and audit review for investigations and incident response
steps.purpose: Locate controls that mandate log analysis, centralized log management, and using logs for incident response and investigations.

step.search_query: Audit logging, event logging, and audit record generation and retention
step.purpose: Find controls that require generating, protecting, reviewing, and retaining audit logs and events.

documents.title: Audit Record Generation
Audit Record Retention
documents.description: Retain audit records for [Assignment: time period] to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of incidents and to meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

documents.title: Event Logging
Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
documents.description:
Provide and implement an audit record reduction and report generation capability that:
  a. Supports on-demand audit record review, analysis, and reporting requirements and after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and
  b. Does not alter the original content or time ordering of audit records.

documents.title: Auditing Capability for Significant Events
documents.description: Upon detection of a potential integrity violation, provide the capability to audit the event and initiate the following actions: [Selection (one or more): generate an audit record; alert current user; alert [Assignment: personnel or roles] ; [Assignment: other actions] ].

step.search_query: Continuous monitoring, security monitoring, and detection of unauthorized activity
step.purpose: Identify controls that require ongoing system and security monitoring, intrusion detection, and alerting.

documents.title: Detection and Monitoring
documents.description:
(a) Employ the following monitoring tools to detect indicators of denial-of-service attacks against, or launched from, the system: [Assignment: monitoring tools] ; and
(b) Monitor the following system resources to determine if sufficient resources exist to prevent effective denial-of-service attacks: [Assignment: system resources].

documents.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
documents.description:
a. Develop a plan of action and milestones for the system to document the planned remediation actions of the organization to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during the assessment of the controls and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities in the system; and
b. Update existing plan of action and milestones [Assignment: frequency] based on the findings from control assessments, independent audits or reviews, and continuous monitoring activities.

documents.title: Testing, Training, and Monitoring
documents.description:
a. Implement a process for ensuring that organizational plans for conducting security and privacy testing, training, and monitoring activities associated with organizational systems:
  1. Are developed and maintained; and
  2. Continue to be executed; and
b. Review testing, training, and monitoring plans for consistency with the organizational risk management strategy and organization-wide priorities for risk response actions.

documents.title: System Monitoring
Continuous Monitoring Strategy

step.purpose: Locate controls that mandate log analysis, centralized log management, and using logs for incident response and investigations.

documents.title: Monitoring Physical Access
Event Logging
Review Historic Audit Logs
documents.description: Review historic audit logs to determine if a vulnerability identified in a [Assignment: system] has been previously exploited within an [Assignment: time period].

documents.title: Central Review and Analysis
documents.description: Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

documents.title: Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: Audit Record Generation

documents.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
documents.description:
a. Develop a plan of action and milestones for the system to document the planned remediation actions of the organization to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during the assessment of the controls and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities in the system; and
b. Update existing plan of action and milestones [Assignment: frequency] based on the findings from control assessments, independent audits or reviews, and continuous monitoring activities.

a. Implement a process for ensuring that organizational plans for conducting security and privacy testing, training, and monitoring activities associated with organizational systems:
  1. Are developed and maintained; and
  2. Continue to be executed; and

documents.title: Central Review and Analysis
documents.description: Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

documents.title: Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: System Monitoring
Continuous Monitoring Strategy

response.answer:
The supplied controls identify several policy/plan areas that relate to logging and monitoring. Briefly:

- Event logging policy: Define what event types the system can log, which organization-defined event types will be logged, logging frequency/situations, rationale for adequacy for after‑the‑fact investigations, and periodic review/update of those event types [au-2 a–e].

- Audit record generation policy: Provide capability for generating audit records for the identified event types, let authorized personnel/roles select which event types components log, and ensure generated records include required content [au-12 a–c].

- Audit record retention policy: Specify retention period for audit records to support after‑the‑fact investigations and meet regulatory/organizational retention requirements [au-11].

- Audit review/analysis and reporting policy: Provide audit record reduction and report generation capabilities that support on‑demand review, analysis, and reporting and that preserve original content/time ordering; and provide central review/analysis capability across system components [au-7; au-6.4].

- Historic log review policy: Review historic audit logs to determine whether identified vulnerabilities have been previously exploited within a defined time period [ra-5.8].

- Monitoring and detection policy: Define monitoring objectives, techniques/methods to detect attacks, indicators, unauthorized connections, when and how to deploy internal/ad hoc monitoring, analysis of detected events/anomalies, and when to adjust monitoring levels as risk changes; also provide monitoring information to designated personnel/roles [si-4 a–g].

- Auditing capability for significant events: On detection of potential integrity violations, specify actions such as generating audit records and alerting users or personnel/roles [si-7.8].

- Denial‑of‑service monitoring policy: Specify monitoring tools to detect DoS indicators and which system resources to monitor to assess DoS susceptibility [sc-5.3 a–b].

- Physical access logging/monitoring policy: Monitor physical access to facilities, review physical access logs on a defined frequency or upon defined events, and coordinate with incident response [pe-6 a–c].

- Maintenance logging and review policy: Log organization‑defined audit events for nonlocal maintenance/diagnostic sessions and review those audit records to detect anomalous behavior [ma-4.1 a–b].

- Continuous monitoring strategy and reporting policy: Establish organization‑wide metrics to monitor, set monitoring/assessment frequencies, perform ongoing monitoring, correlate/analysis of assessment and monitoring information, define response actions, and report security/privacy status to designated personnel at defined frequency [pm-31 a–f].

- Testing/training/monitoring process: Ensure plans for conducting security/privacy testing, training, and monitoring are developed, maintained, executed, and reviewed for consistency with risk strategy and priorities [pm-14 a–b].

If you want, I can map these policy areas into specific policy statements or a template for each (e.g., required content, responsible roles, review cadence) using only these controls.

plan.original_query: What is the capital of France?

steps.purpose: Find any control statements about managing or specifying national capitals or geographic administrative data — to see if the control catalog contains references to country capitals.

step.purpose: Find any control statements about managing or specifying national capitals or geographic administrative data — to see if the control catalog contains references to country capitals.

response.answer: I could not find any NIST SP 800-53 control relevant to that question in the policy library, so I cannot answer it. Try rephrasing the question in terms of a security control, a control family, or a control ID such as AC-2.
